mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate declines below 0.50% signal monetary accommodation that reduces equity discount rates and increases present value of future corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.37% as of May 2016 indicates ultra-low rate environment
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS < 0.50%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month payroll employment growth above 150,000 persons signals robust labor market demand that sustains consumer spending and corporate profit growth, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 143,894 thousand as of May 2016
failure_condition: Correlation between month-over-month PAYEMS growth and 60-day forward SPX returns is not significantly positive (Pearson r < 0.15, p > 0.05) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts decline below 1,100 thousand units signals weakening residential investment and forward-looking demand contraction, predicting negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as equity markets reprice growth expectations downward.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1,172 thousand as of April 2016
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of instances when HOUST < 1100, or Rank IC < 0.01 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production index declines month-over-month while unemployment rate rises simultaneously, signaling stagflationary pressure that compresses equity valuations through simultaneous margin compression and discount rate expansion, producing negative 20-day returns.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines 0.5% MoM AND UNRATE rises 0.2pp MoM
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 103.55 and UNRATE at 4.7% as of May 2016
failure_condition: Joint occurrence of INDPRO decline and UNRATE rise does not predict negative 20-day returns (mean return > 0% in triggered instances, t-test p > 0.10), or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Real retail sales growth decelerates below 2% annualized rate while housing starts simultaneously fall below 1,150 thousand units, indicating synchronized demand weakness across consumer and residential sectors that predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 455,644 million and HOUST at 1,172 thousand as of May 2016
regime_hint: synchronized demand contraction across consumer and residential
failure_condition: Interaction term between RSAFS growth and HOUST level does not significantly predict 60-day returns (p > 0.05), or SPX produces positive returns in more than 50% of dual-trigger instances.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation measured by CPIAUCSL rises above 2.5% year-over-year while federal funds rate remains below 1.0%, signaling real rate compression that incentivizes equity allocation over cash, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 238.89 and FEDFUNDS at 0.37% as of April-May 2016
regime_hint: low real rates with moderate inflation
failure_condition: Real rate compression (FEDFUNDS - CPIAUCSL YoY) does not correlate positively with 20-day SPX returns (Pearson r < 0.12, p > 0.05), or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate rises above 5.0% signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer confidence and discretionary spending, predicting negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as equity markets reprice earnings expectations downward.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.7% as of May 2016, below 5.0% threshold
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or recession onset
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of instances when UNRATE > 5.0%, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production index rises above 104.0 while real retail sales simultaneously grow above 3% annualized, signaling synchronized supply-side strength and demand resilience that produces positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days through earnings growth expectations.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 103.55 and RSAFS at 455,644 million as of May 2016
failure_condition: Interaction term between INDPRO and RSAFS growth does not significantly predict 5-day returns (p > 0.05), or SPX produces negative returns in more than 50% of dual-trigger instances.
